entries.definition:
كتاب الجيم

جه

ينْهَضْنَ بالهُنْدُوانيّاتِ والجُنَنِ

حيث تَرَى الخيلَ بالأبطال عابِسَةً

والجِنّة: الجنون؛ وذلك أنّه يغطِّي العقل. وجَنَانُ الليل: سوادُه وسَتْرُه الأشياءَ. قال:

بذِي الرِّمْث والأرْطَى عِياضَ بنَ ناشِبِ

ولولا جَنَان الليل أدْرَكَ ركْضُنا

ويقال جُنُون الليل، والمعنى واحد. ويقال جُنَّ النَّبتُ جُنوناً إذا اشتدّ وخَرَج زهره. فهذا يمكن أن يكون من الجُنونِ استعارةً كما يُجنُّ الإنسان فيهيج، ثم يكون أصل الجنون ما ذكرناه من السَّتْر. والقياس صحيح. وجَنَان النّاس مُعْظمُهم، ويسمَّى السَّوَادَ. والمَجَنّة الجنون. فأمّا الحيّة الذي يسمَّى الجانَّ فهو تشبيهٌ له بالواحد من الجانّ. والجنُّ سُمُّوا بذلك لأنهم متستِّرون عن أعيُنِ الخَلْق. قال الله تعالى: ] إِنَّهُ يَرَاكُمْ هُوَ وَقَبِيلُهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لا تَرَوْنَهُمْ [ [الأعراف 27]. والجناجنِ: عظام الصَّدْر.

(جه) الجيم والهاء ليس أصلاً؛ لأنه صوتٌ. يقال جهجهت بالسَّبُع إذا صحتَ به. قال:

* فجاء دُونَ الزَّجرِ والتجهجُهِ *

جو - جأ - جب

وحَكَى ناسٌ: تجهجَهَ عن الأمر انتهى. وهذا إن كان صحيحاً فهو في باب المقابلة؛ لأنك تقول جَهْجَهْتُ به فتجَهْجَهَ.

ـ/د
جُنَّة junnatun [n.] shield, cover, protection (58:16) اتَّخَذُوا أَيْمَانَهُمْ جُنَّةً they have used their oaths as a cover for [their false deeds].

أَجِنَّة ʾajinnatun [pl. of جَنِين janīn] foetuses, something hidden (53:32) وَإِذْ أَنْتُمْ أَجِنَّةٌ فِي بُطُونِ أُمَّهَاتِكُمْ and when you were foetuses in the bellies of your mothers.
entries.definition_english:
ـ/د
جُنَّة junnatun [n.] shield, cover, protection (58:16) اتَّخَذُوا أَيْمَانَهُمْ جُنَّةً they have used their oaths as a cover for [their false deeds].

أَجِنَّة ʾajinnatun [pl. of جَنِين janīn] foetuses, something hidden (53:32) وَإِذْ أَنْتُمْ أَجِنَّةٌ فِي بُطُونِ أُمَّهَاتِكُمْ and when you were foetuses in the bellies of your mothers.

entries.definition:
ـ/د j–h–d barren land, desert land; hardship, strive, to tire oneself, to exert oneself; ability, capacity, exertion; to become emaciated, to squander one’s wealth. Of this root, five forms occur in 41 places in the Qurʾan: جَاهَدَ jāhada 27 times; جَهْد jahd five times; جُهْد juhd once; جِهَاد jihād four times and مُجَاهِدُون mujāhidūn four times.
جَاهَدَ jāhada I [v. III, intrans. with prep. فِي] to strive, to endeavour, to fight (8:74) وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَهَاجَرُوا وَجَاهَدُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ those who believed and emigrated, and struggled in God’s cause II [trans.] to urge; to argue, to dispute with, to put under pressure (31:15) وَإِنْ جَاهَدَاكَ عَلَى أَنْ تُشْرِكَ بِي مَا لَيْسَ لَكَ بِهِ عِلْمٌ فَلَا تُطِعْهُمَا but if they pressure you to associate with Me anything you do not know [to be deserving], do not obey them.

جَهْد jahd [v. n. used adverbially] the utmost of, the most possible, to the limit (16:38) وَأَقْسَمُوا بِاللَّهِ جَهْدَ أَيْمَانِهِمْ they have sworn by God with the utmost of their oaths.

جُهْد juhd [n./v. n.] capacity, power, ability, extreme effort *(9:79) وَالَّذِينَ لَا يَجِدُونَ إِلَّا جُهْدَهُمْ those who find nothing [to give for charity] but [the very little they can spare of] the utmost effort.

178
entries.definition_english:

III, intrans. with prep. فِي] to strive, to endeavour, to fight (8:74) وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَهَاجَرُوا وَجَاهَدُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ those who believed and emigrated, and struggled in God’s cause II [trans.] to urge; to argue, to dispute with, to put under pressure (31:15) وَإِنْ جَاهَدَاكَ عَلَى أَنْ تُشْرِكَ بِي مَا لَيْسَ لَكَ بِهِ عِلْمٌ فَلَا تُطِعْهُمَا but if they pressure you to associate with Me anything you do not know [to be deserving], do not obey them.

178

entries.definition:
ـ/ر
charity] but [the very little they can spare of] the utmost effort.

جِهَاد jihād [v. n./n.] striving, struggling, fighting (22:78) وَجَاهِدُوا فِي اللَّهِ حَقَّ جِهَادِهِ strive for God’s cause as striving for Him is due.

مُجَاهِدُون mujāhidūn [pl. of act. part. مُجَاهِد mujāhid] those who strive, those who fight (47:31) وَلَنَبْلُوَنَّكُمْ حَتَّى نَعْلَمَ الْمُجَاهِدِينَ مِنْكُمْ وَالصَّابِرِينَ We will try you until We know those who strive among you, and the steadfast.
entries.definition_english:
ـ/ر
charity] but [the very little they can spare of] the utmost effort.

جِهَاد jihād [v. n./n.] striving, struggling, fighting (22:78) وَجَاهِدُوا فِي اللَّهِ حَقَّ جِهَادِهِ strive for God’s cause as striving for Him is due.

مُجَاهِدُون mujāhidūn [pl. of act. part. مُجَاهِد mujāhid] those who strive, those who fight (47:31) وَلَنَبْلُوَنَّكُمْ حَتَّى نَعْلَمَ الْمُجَاهِدِينَ مِنْكُمْ وَالصَّابِرِينَ We will try you until We know those who strive among you, and the steadfast.

entries.definition:
ـ/ر j–h–r plain treeless land; the outer appearance of a person, appearance; to declare, to say openly, to be loud, to be open, a loud voice; to be pure. Of this root, four forms occur 16 times in the Qurʾan: جَهَرَ jahara five times; جَهْر jahr seven times; جَهْرَة jahratan three times and جِهَارًا jihāran once.
جَهَرَ jahara a [v. intrans. with prep. بِ] 1 to speak loudly (17:110) وَلَا تَجْهَرْ بِصَلَاتِكَ وَلَا تُخَافِتْ بِهَا وَابْتَغِ بَيْنَ ذَلِكَ سَبِيلًا and do not be loud in your prayer, or too quiet, but seek between this a way 2 to speak openly (13:10) سَوَاءٌ مِنْكُمْ مَنْ أَسَرَّ الْقَوْلَ وَمَنْ جَهَرَ بِهِ alike among you is the one who conceals his words and the one who speaks them openly.

جَهْر jahr [n./v. n.] I speaking loudly, raising the voice (7:205) وَاذْكُرْ رَبَّكَ فِي نَفْسِكَ تَضَرُّعًا وَخِيفَةً وَدُونَ الْجَهْرِ مِنَ الْقَوْلِ and remember your Lord, in your soul imploringly and in awe, and in subdued tones [lit. without loudness] 2 speaking openly (21:110) إِنَّهُ يَعْلَمُ الْجَهْرَ مِنَ الْقَوْلِ but He knows what is said openly and He knows what you conceal 3 divulging, announcing, declaring, disclosure, telling (4:148) لَا يُحِبُّ اللَّهُ الْجَهْرَ بِالسُّوءِ مِنَ الْقَوْلِ إِلَّا مَنْ ظُلِمَ God does not like the publicising of evil except [by] someone who has been wronged.

جَهْرَة jahratan [v. n. used adverbially] 1 openly, visibly (2:55) لَنْ نُؤْمِنَ لَكَ حَتَّى نَرَى اللَّهَ جَهْرَةً we will not believe you until we see God visibly 2 expectedly, after a warning (6:47) إِنْ أَتَاكُمْ عَذَابُ اللَّهِ بَغْتَةً أَوْ جَهْرَةً if the punishment of God should come to you suddenly or with warning.

جِهَارًا jihāran [v. n. used adverbially] openly (71:8) إِنِّي دَعَوْتُهُمْ جِهَارًا I have called them openly/aloud.

179
entries.definition_english:

جِهَارًا jihāran [v. n. used adverbially] openly (71:8) إِنِّي دَعَوْتُهُمْ جِهَارًا I have called them openly/aloud.

179

entries.definition:
ـ/ز j–h–z equipment, gear, rig; to fit out, to prepare, to equip, to make ready; to finish off, to be quick and light-footed. Of this root, two forms occur twice each in the Qurʾan: جَهَّزَ jahhaza and جِهَاز/جَهَاز jihāz/jahāz.
جَهَّزَ jahhaza [v. II, trans.] to equip with necessary gear, to rig up, to provide with necessary provisions (12:70) فَلَمَّا جَهَّزَهُمْ بِجَهَازِهِمْ and when he provided them with their provisions, he placed the drinking-cup in the pack of his brother.

جِهَاز/جَهَاز jihāz/jahāz [n.] gears, supplies, provisions (12:59) وَلَمَّا جَهَّزَهُمْ بِجَهَازِهِمْ قَالَ ائْتُونِي بِأَخٍ لَكُمْ مِنْ أَبِيكُمْ and when he had provided them with their provisions, he said, ‘Bring me a [half] brother of yours from your father[’s side].’
entries.definition_english:
ـ/ز j–h–z equipment, gear, rig; to fit out, to prepare, to equip, to make ready; to finish off, to be quick and light-footed. Of this root, two forms occur twice each in the Qurʾan: جَهَّزَ jahhaza and جِهَاز/جَهَاز jihāz/jahāz.
جَهَّزَ jahhaza [v. II, trans.] to equip with necessary gear, to rig up, to provide with necessary provisions (12:70) فَلَمَّا جَهَّزَهُمْ بِجَهَازِهِمْ and when he provided them with their provisions, he placed the drinking-cup in the pack of his brother.

جِهَاز/جَهَاز jihāz/jahāz [n.] gears, supplies, provisions (12:59) وَلَمَّا جَهَّزَهُمْ بِجَهَازِهِمْ قَالَ ائْتُونِي بِأَخٍ لَكُمْ مِنْ أَبِيكُمْ and when he had provided them with their provisions, he said, ‘Bring me a [half] brother of yours from your father[’s side].’

entries.definition:
ـ/ل j–h–l ignorance, lack of knowledge, to ignore; foolishness, quick temper, to be peevish, to be rash. Of this root, six forms occur 24 times in the Qurʾan: تَجْهَل tajhal five times; جَاهِل jāhil once; جَاهِلُون jāhilūn nine times; جَهُول jahūl once; جَهَالَة jahālatun four times and جَاهِلِيَّة jāhiliyyatun four times.
تَجْهَل tajhal [imperf. of v. trans. with no object] to be devoid of knowledge, to have no information, not to know; to be foolish (7:138) قَالَ إِنَّكُمْ قَوْمٌ تَجْهَلُونَ they said, ‘Moses, make for us a god as they have gods.’ He said, ‘Surely you are a people who do not know.’

جَاهِل jāhil [act. part., pl. جَاهِلُون jāhilūn] 1 ignorant (2:67) قَالُوا أَتَتَّخِذُنَا هُزُوًا قَالَ أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ أَنْ أَكُونَ مِنَ الْجَاهِلِينَ they said, ‘Are you making fun of us?’, he answered, ‘God forbid that I should be so ignorant’ 2 uninformed, undiscerning, unaware (2:273) يَحْسَبُهُمُ الْجَاهِلُ أَغْنِيَاءَ مِنَ التَّعَفُّفِ the undiscerning might think them rich because of their self-restraint [from showing how needy they are] 3 the angry, aggressors, abusers; foolish (25:63) وَإِذَا خَاطَبَهُمُ الْجَاهِلُونَ قَالُوا سَلَامًا and who, when the abusers address them, say, ‘Peace.’

جَهُول jahūl [intens. act. part.] very rash, very foolish; ignominious (33:72) وَلَٰكِنْ حَمَلَهَا الْإِنْسَانُ إِنَّهُ كَانَ ظَلُومًا جَهُولًا but man carried it–indeed he is sinful, very foolish.

جَهَالَة jahālatun [n. state of complete unawareness, ignorance, 

َهَنَّم

foolishness (6:54) مَنْ عَمِلَ مِنْكُمْ سُوءًا بِجَهَالَةٍ ثُمَّ تَابَ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ وَأَصْلَحَ فَأَنَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ whoever of you does evil in ignorance, and thereafter repents and makes amends, God is All-Forgiving, All-Compassionate.

ٱلْجَاهِلِيَّة al-Jāhiliyyatu [proper name] (normally associated with lawlessness) the time prior to the coming of Islam in Arabia, the state of pagan ignorance (5:50) أَفَحُكْمَ ٱلْجَاهِلِيَّةِ يَبْغُونَ do they want judgement according to the time prior to the coming of Islam in Arabia [lit. the state of pagan ignorance]?

جَهَنَّم jahannam [a borrowing from Hebrew (also thought to be from Persian) occurring 77 times in the Qurʾan] Hell (25:65) رَبَّنَا ٱصْرِفْ عَنَّا عَذَابَ جَهَنَّمَ our Lord, turn away from us the chastisement of Hell.

جَهَنَّم jahannam [a borrowing from Hebrew (also thought to be from Persian) occurring 77 times in the Qurʾan] Hell (25:65) رَبَّنَا ٱصْرِفْ عَنَّا عَذَابَ جَهَنَّمَ our Lord, turn away from us the chastisement of Hell.
